ComputerBasics.q: What is the very first topic taught?
ComputerBasics.a: Operating systems layout: explaining folders, the taskbar, active windows, and hardware mouse inputs.
Students must learn to keep files neat, name files appropriately, and navigate local directories independently.
Yes, we teach critical shortcuts like Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V, Alt+Tab, and Win+D to build desktop navigation speed.

ComputerBasics.q: Is this suitable for absolute beginners?
ComputerBasics.a: Yes, this course assumes no prior computer knowledge and starts from the basics.

TouchTyping.q: How does touch typing differ from normal typing?
TouchTyping.a: Touch typing relies on muscle memory and using all ten fingers on the home row, without looking down at the keyboard.

TouchTyping.q: What typing speed can a child achieve in 6 weeks?
TouchTyping.a: While it varies, the average student improves from under 15 WPM to 35-40+ WPM with practice.

TouchTyping.q: Do we need a special keyboard?
TouchTyping.a: No, any standard external keyboard or laptop keyboard works perfectly.
We recommend 10-15 minutes of daily practice using our tools to build muscle memory.

CodingScratch.a: Scratch is a block-based visual programming language developed by MIT that teaches coding logic without syntax errors.

CodingScratch.q: What logic concepts will my child learn?
CodingScratch.a: Key computer science concepts: variables, loops, conditional statements, coordinates (X, Y), and algorithms.

CodingScratch.q: Can my child play their games after the course?
CodingScratch.a: Yes! Scratch games are hosted online and can be shared with friends and family to play anytime.

GenerativeAI.q: What AI tools will students use?
GenerativeAI.a: Students learn prompting structures using safe educational frontends and tools like Canva's AI design features.

GenerativeAI.q: Will my child learn prompt engineering?
GenerativeAI.a: Yes, they learn templates utilizing Roles, Tasks, Context, and Output parameters to draft precise queries.

SpreadsheetsExcel.q: Is Excel too advanced for class 6 students?
SpreadsheetsExcel.a: No, we teach spreadsheet grids visually, showing how cells act as mathematical boxes to make math intuitive.
Basic arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication) and functions like SUM and AVERAGE.

CyberSafety.q: Why is cyber hygiene crucial for kids?
CyberSafety.a: With online gaming and school accounts, kids are target profiles for password cracking, phishing scams, and track cookie capture.

CyberSafety.q: What will my child do in the safety course?
CyberSafety.a: They will learn to build secure passwords, spot scam links, adjust browser privacy settings, and practice safe sharing.
